summary.headline: Self-Distilled RLVR achieves 84 upvotes challenging teacher-student distillation; CORAL introduces autonomous multi-agent evolution for open-ended discovery; SimpleStream baseline embarrasses complex streaming video methods
summary.body:
April 7th is headlined by a striking result in LLM training methodology: **Self-Distilled RLVR** demonstrates that a model can serve as both teacher and student in on-policy self-distillation, achieving competitive results without requiring a larger teacher model. With 84 upvotes, it's the most engaged paper of the day, signaling broad community interest in simplifying the distillation-RL pipeline. **CORAL** complements this with autonomous multi-agent evolution replacing rigid control heuristics with long-running agents that explore, reflect, and collaborate through shared knowledge — a genuinely novel framework for open-ended AI discovery.

The video and multimodal space delivers two important simplicity results: **SimpleStream** shows that a sliding-window baseline feeding recent frames to an off-the-shelf VLM matches or surpasses 13 published streaming models (56 upvotes), while **Token Warping** demonstrates that warping tokens rather than pixels helps MLLMs handle viewpoint changes (23 upvotes). Both papers challenge the trend toward ever-more-complex architectures by showing simpler approaches can dominate.

The model landscape continues its **Gemma-4 domination** with 7 variants in the trending top 20, while **Qwen3.5 distilled from Claude Opus** models generate massive download numbers (548k+). GitHub sees **openscreen** explode to 1,838 stars/day, and **NousResearch/hermes-agent** and **Google AI Edge Gallery** continue their strong upward trajectories, confirming the AI developer tooling boom shows no signs of slowing.

researcher_notes:
**Self-Distilled RLVR is the most consequential training methodology paper today.** The core insight — that RLVR's sparse verifiable reward signals can replace a teacher model's dense signals in self-distillation — challenges the assumption that distillation requires a capability gap between teacher and student. At 84 upvotes, the community clearly sees the practical implications: eliminating the need for a larger teacher model during distillation dramatically reduces the compute requirements for RL-based training. Watch for rapid adoption in the open-weight community, where access to large teacher models is often the bottleneck.

**The 'simplicity wins' theme across two domains is not coincidental.** SimpleStream's embarrassing baseline result (sliding window > 13 complex streaming methods) and Token Warping's elegant approach (warp tokens not pixels for viewpoint changes) both suggest the field has been over-engineering solutions in video understanding. This pattern — where simpler baselines outperform complex architectures once properly evaluated — typically signals that benchmark design, not model architecture, was the real bottleneck. Expect follow-up work to focus on harder benchmarks rather than more complex methods.

**CORAL represents a genuine paradigm shift in multi-agent systems.** Unlike existing multi-agent frameworks that rely on fixed heuristics and hard-coded exploration rules, CORAL's agents autonomously decide when to explore, reflect, and collaborate through shared persistent knowledge. The 43-upvote engagement level for a systems paper (typically lower than methods papers) indicates strong interest in autonomous agent coordination. The connection to real-world agent systems like those being built by NousResearch (hermes-agent, 1,574 stars/day on GitHub) is direct.

**'Therefore I am. I Think' is the sleeper hit that deserves more attention.** The finding that reasoning models commit to decisions before generating their chain-of-thought — detectable via linear probes on pre-generation activations — fundamentally questions whether CoT is reasoning or rationalization. At 28 upvotes, it's well-received but not viral; the implications for interpretability and alignment are potentially enormous. If models decide first and think second, current alignment approaches that target the reasoning process may be targeting the wrong stage.

**The Gemma-4 vs. Qwen3.5 ecosystem race is accelerating.** Gemma-4 holds 7 of 20 trending model slots (31B-it, 26B-A4B, E4B, E2B, NVFP4, two Unsloth GGUFs), but Qwen3.5 distilled from Claude Opus variants are generating extraordinary engagement (2,403 likes, 548k downloads for the 27B variant). The Claude-distilled models represent a fascinating meta-trend: open-weight models trained on closed-model outputs, creating a capability transfer pipeline from proprietary to open ecosystems.

papers.title: Self-Distilled RLVR
papers.summary: Demonstrates that on-policy self-distillation — where the same model serves as both teacher and student — achieves competitive results with RLVR, eliminating the need for a larger teacher model in distillation-based training.
key_findings: Self-distillation matches or exceeds standard teacher-student distillation for reasoning tasks
RLVR's sparse verifiable rewards provide sufficient signal for self-distillation without teacher
Eliminates the compute overhead of maintaining a separate larger teacher model during training

papers.title: A Simple Baseline for Streaming Video Understanding
papers.summary: Formalizes SimpleStream, a sliding-window baseline that feeds the most recent N frames to an off-the-shelf VLM, demonstrating it matches or surpasses 13 major offline and online video LLM baselines on standard benchmarks.
key_findings: A sliding-window approach using recent frames matches or surpasses 13 published streaming models
Complex memory mechanisms are unnecessary for current streaming video understanding benchmarks
The result suggests current benchmarks may not adequately test long-term temporal reasoning

papers.title: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
papers.summary: First framework for autonomous multi-agent evolution on open-ended problems, replacing rigid heuristics with long-running agents that explore, reflect, and collaborate through shared persistent knowledge.
key_findings: Autonomous agents outperform fixed-heuristic approaches on open-ended discovery tasks
Shared persistent knowledge enables effective inter-agent collaboration without hard-coded rules
The framework demonstrates sustained progress on problems requiring long-horizon exploration

papers.title: Therefore I am. I Think
papers.summary: Presents evidence that reasoning models commit to decisions before generating chain-of-thought, with linear probes successfully decoding tool-calling decisions from pre-generation activations with very high confidence.
key_findings: Linear probes decode tool-calling decisions from pre-generation activations before any reasoning tokens
Detectable early-encoded decisions shape chain-of-thought rather than arising from it
Findings challenge the assumption that CoT is genuine step-by-step reasoning rather than post-hoc rationalization

papers.title: NearID: Identity Representation Learning via Near-identity Distractors
affiliations: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
papers.summary: Introduces the first principled framework for identity-focused evaluation using Near-identity distractors that eliminate contextual shortcuts, revealing that existing vision encoders entangle object identity with background context.
key_findings: Existing vision encoders unreliably entangle object identity with background context
Near-identity distractors on identical backgrounds isolate true identity signals
Framework enables more reliable evaluation of personalized generation and image editing

papers.title: Token Warping Helps MLLMs Look from Nearby Viewpoints
affiliations: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology
papers.summary: Demonstrates that warping tokens rather than pixels helps multimodal LLMs handle viewpoint changes, drawing on mental imagery theories of part-level structural representations for perspective transformation.
key_findings: Token-level warping is more robust to depth estimation errors than pixel-level warping
Part-level structural representations better support perspective transformation in MLLMs
The approach significantly improves MLLM performance on novel viewpoint reasoning tasks

papers.title: Test-Time Scaling Makes Overtraining Compute-Optimal
papers.summary: Introduces Train-to-Test (T^2) scaling laws that jointly optimize model size, training tokens, and inference samples under fixed end-to-end budgets, modernizing Chinchilla-style pretraining laws for the test-time scaling era.
key_findings: Chinchilla scaling laws are suboptimal when test-time compute is factored in
Smaller overtrained models + more test-time samples often beat larger Chinchilla-optimal models
T^2 scaling laws provide practical guidance for joint train-inference budget allocation

papers.title: Communicating about Space: Language-Mediated Spatial Integration Across Partial Views
papers.summary: Introduces COSMIC benchmark for collaborative spatial communication, testing whether MLLMs can align distinct egocentric views through dialogue to form coherent allocentric mental models of shared environments.
key_findings: MLLMs struggle to build shared spatial mental models from multiple egocentric viewpoints
Dialogue-based spatial integration is significantly harder than single-view spatial reasoning
The COSMIC benchmark reveals a fundamental gap in current MLLM spatial communication abilities

papers.title: Video Models Reason Early: Exploiting Plan Commitment for Maze Solving
papers.summary: Reveals that video diffusion models commit to a high-level motion plan within the first few denoising steps during maze solving, providing the first mechanistic understanding of reasoning dynamics in video generation models.
key_findings: Video diffusion models commit to motion plans in early denoising steps
After early commitment, later steps refine but do not fundamentally alter the plan
Early plan commitment enables exploitable shortcuts for test-time inference optimization

papers.title: AIBench: Evaluating Visual-Logical Consistency in Academic Illustration Generation
papers.summary: First benchmark using VQA to evaluate logical correctness in AI-generated academic illustrations, addressing the gap between general image generation quality and domain-specific logical consistency requirements.
key_findings: State-of-the-art image generation models frequently produce logically inconsistent academic illustrations
VQA-based evaluation is more reliable than direct VLM comparison for complex illustrations
Current models struggle with diagram consistency even when visual quality appears high

papers.title: GrandCode: Achieving Grandmaster Level in Competitive Programming via Agentic Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: A multi-agent RL system for competitive programming that orchestrates multiple specialized agents to achieve grandmaster-level performance, surpassing Google Gemini 3 Deep Think's previous best result.
key_findings: Multi-agent RL orchestration achieves grandmaster-level competitive programming performance
System surpasses previous best AI result (Gemini 3 Deep Think, 8th place) under live conditions
Agent specialization (problem decomposition, coding, debugging) is key to competitive performance

papers.title: Forecasting Supply Chain Disruptions with Foresight Learning
papers.summary: An end-to-end framework training LLMs to produce calibrated probabilistic forecasts of supply chain disruptions using realized disruption outcomes as supervision, targeting infrequent high-impact events.
key_findings: Task-specific adaptation dramatically improves LLM calibration for rare event forecasting
Realized disruption outcome supervision outperforms general-purpose LLM reasoning
Framework produces actionable probabilistic forecasts for firms and policymakers

papers.title: Ask or Assume? Uncertainty-Aware Clarification-Seeking in Coding Agents
papers.summary: Systematic evaluation of clarification-seeking abilities in LLM coding agents on underspecified tasks, proposing uncertainty-aware strategies that balance autonomous execution with asking clarifying questions.
key_findings: Current coding agents rarely ask clarifying questions even when instructions are severely underspecified
Uncertainty-aware clarification strategies significantly improve task completion on ambiguous specs
There exists an optimal trade-off between autonomous execution and clarification-seeking frequency

papers.title: AgentSocialBench: Evaluating Privacy Risks in Human-Centered Agentic Social Networks
papers.summary: Evaluates privacy risks in agentic social networks where collaborative AI agents serve individual users across domains, addressing novel challenges of cross-domain information leakage and inter-agent privacy mediation.
key_findings: Agentic social networks create novel cross-domain privacy leakage vectors
Current agent frameworks lack adequate privacy mediation between user-serving agents
Human-centered agent networks require fundamentally different privacy models than isolated agents

models.name: Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled
models.description: Qwen3.5-27B distilled from Claude 4.6 Opus reasoning outputs, achieving remarkable engagement with 2,403 likes and 548k downloads — the most engaged community model this week.

models.name: Gemma-4-31B-IT
models.description: Google's flagship 31B dense Gemma-4 instruction-tuned model, the most downloaded Gemma-4 variant with strong multimodal image-text-to-text capabilities.

models.name: Qianfan-OCR
models.description: Baidu's vision-language OCR model built on InternVL architecture, achieving 1,029 likes and positioning as a strong open-weight alternative for document understanding.

models.name: Cohere Transcribe 03-2026
models.description: Cohere's latest automatic speech recognition model with 818 likes and 128k downloads, entering the open ASR space alongside Whisper and other established models.

models.name: Gemma-4-31B-JANG_4M-CRACK
models.description: Abliterated (uncensored) Gemma-4-31B variant in MLX format, demonstrating the rapid community customization pipeline from model release to uncensored variant.

models.name: Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled-v2-GGUF
models.description: GGUF-quantized version of the Claude Opus distilled Qwen3.5, enabling local deployment with 272k downloads and 521 likes.

models.name: Bonsai-8B-GGUF
models.description: 1-bit quantized 8B model pushing the extreme edge of model compression for resource-constrained deployment.

models.name: Gemma-4-26B-A4B-IT
models.description: Gemma-4 mixture-of-experts variant with 26B total / 4B active parameters, offering strong multimodal performance at reduced inference cost.

models.name: VOID Model
models.description: Physics-aware video object removal model based on CogVideoX, generating strong anticipatory engagement with 455 likes despite 0 downloads (likely gated release).

models.name: Gemma-4-E4B-IT
models.description: Gemma-4 any-to-any model at 4B-equivalent scale, supporting multimodal input and output in a compact form factor.

models.name: OmniVoice
models.description: Zero-shot multilingual voice cloning model supporting 650+ languages, entering the open-weight TTS space with strong community reception (301 likes).

models.name: LFM2.5-350M
models.description: Non-transformer architecture (liquid state machines) at 350M scale, targeting edge deployment with a fundamentally different compute paradigm.

trending_repos.name: siddharthvaddem/openscreen
trending_repos.description: Open-source screen recording and demo creation tool, free for commercial use with no watermarks. An alternative to Screen Studio that has gone viral with 1,838 stars/day.

trending_repos.name: NousResearch/hermes-agent
trending_repos.description: NousResearch's extensible AI agent framework that grows with users, trending at 1,574 stars/day. Backed by NousResearch's model-native expertise.

trending_repos.name: google-ai-edge/gallery
trending_repos.description: Google's showcase gallery for on-device ML/GenAI use cases, enabling local model experimentation. Trending at 1,107 stars/day, confirming mainstream interest in on-device AI.

trending_repos.name: abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus
trending_repos.description: Client-side knowledge graph creator for code repositories that runs entirely in the browser. Drop in a GitHub repo and get an interactive knowledge graph with built-in Graph RAG agent.

trending_repos.name: KeygraphHQ/shannon
trending_repos.description: Autonomous white-box AI pentester for web applications and APIs. Analyzes source code, identifies attack vectors, and executes real exploits to prove vulnerabilities.

trending_repos.name: onyx-dot-app/onyx
trending_repos.description: Open source AI platform with advanced chat features that works with every LLM. Trending at 639 stars/day.

trending_repos.name: google-ai-edge/LiteRT-LM
trending_repos.description: Google's lightweight runtime for running language models on edge devices, complementing the AI Edge Gallery with C++ inference infrastructure.

trending_repos.name: kepano/obsidian-skills
trending_repos.description: Agent skills for Obsidian, teaching AI agents to use Markdown, Bases, JSON Canvas, and CLI. Part of the broader agent-skills ecosystem trend.

trending_repos.name: tobi/qmd
trending_repos.description: Mini CLI search engine for personal docs, knowledge bases, and meeting notes. Tracks current SOTA approaches while keeping everything local.

trending_repos.name: NVIDIA/personaplex
trending_repos.description: NVIDIA's PersonaPlex system for generating and managing AI personas, trending at 295 stars/day.

trending_repos.name: Blaizzy/mlx-vlm
trending_repos.description: Package for inference and fine-tuning Vision Language Models on Mac using MLX. Trending at 277 stars/day as Apple Silicon AI development matures.

trending_repos.name: ggml-org/llama.cpp
trending_repos.description: The foundational C/C++ LLM inference engine, steady at 267 stars/day as it continues to add support for latest models.

trending_repos.name: ollama/ollama
trending_repos.description: Leading local LLM runner, now supporting Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, MiniMax, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, and latest Gemma/Qwen models. Steady at 196 stars/day.

trending_repos.name: HKUDS/DeepTutor
trending_repos.description: Agent-native personalized learning assistant from HKU, trending at 164 stars/day.

themes.name: Self-Distillation Without Teachers
themes.description: Self-Distilled RLVR demonstrates that models can serve as their own teachers during distillation, potentially eliminating the need for larger teacher models and democratizing RL-based training.

themes.name: Simplicity Beats Complexity
themes.description: SimpleStream and Token Warping demonstrate that simpler approaches can match or exceed complex architectures in video understanding and viewpoint reasoning, challenging the trend toward ever-more-complex methods.

themes.name: Autonomous Agent Evolution
themes.description: CORAL, hermes-agent, and AgentSocialBench collectively push the frontier of how AI agents self-organize, evolve, and interact in social contexts with appropriate safety guardrails.

themes.name: Reasoning Model Introspection
themes.description: Papers questioning how models actually reason — whether decisions precede CoT, how video models commit to plans early — signal growing interest in mechanistic understanding of model behavior.

themes.name: Agent Safety Infrastructure Maturation
themes.description: AgentSocialBench and AgentHazard from yesterday continue to build out the evaluation infrastructure for AI agent safety, now covering privacy, harmful behavior, and social network dynamics.

themes.name: On-Device and Edge AI Acceleration
themes.description: Google AI Edge Gallery and LiteRT-LM on GitHub, combined with LiquidAI LFM2.5, Bonsai-8B, and mlx-vlm, show accelerating investment in running models locally on consumer hardware.
